title: Refinery feels heat in DSI probe
desc: The Department of Special Investigation (DSI) will summon PC Siam Petroleum in Surat Thani and ask them to clarify allegations of oil hoarding by the company, as the case may be treated as a special case.

title: Pheu Thai plans overhaul of the SSF
desc: The Pheu Thai Party has unveiled a sweeping plan to amend the Social Security Act, promising a structural overhaul of the Social Security Fund (SSF), including a shake-up of its management board, expanded benefits and greater rights for insured workers.

title: Former deputy PM Parnpree set to return as foreign adviser
desc: Former deputy prime minister and foreign minister Parnpree Bahiddha-Nukara is set to return to the Foreign Affairs Ministry as chief adviser to the minister, says a ministry source.

title: Firms eye post-Songkran flight cuts
desc: The aviation regulator expects airlines to cut flights after Songkran as fuel costs rise and travel demand weakens.

title: Influencers, online ads trigger e-vaping rise, worrying parents
desc: Growing concern is mounting over influencers driving youth vaping, with many parents struggling to respond.

title: Public transport operators to protest
desc: Public transport operators in major cities are under mounting pressure as surging fuel prices erode incomes and threaten services.

title: New govt off to a bumpy start
desc: There will be no honeymoon period for the incoming administration of Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul, with academics warning the government has only a narrow window to prove it can manage a deepening energy and cost-of-living crisis -- or risk a steep fall in public support.

title: Thai diesel prices to rise another 2.80 baht/litre
desc: Diesel prices will rise another 2.80 baht a litre at 5am on Sunday as the state Oil Fuel Fund will cut its diesel price subsidy.

title: Bottled drinking water placed on price-control list
desc: The government has added bottled drinking water and seasoning sauces to its price-control list, along with plastic pellets, in order to prevent undue price increases in light of cost pressures arising from the Middle East war.

title: ‘Crypto coach’ facing charges in B30-million fraud
desc: Police have arrested a self-styled cryptocurrency coach and her accomplice accused of luring the public into investing in a fraudulent cryptocurrency scheme, with promises of high returns but blocked withdrawals, causing losses of about 30 million baht.

title: Probe refiners, don’t beg for donations, says Democrats’ Korn
desc: Democrat Party deputy leader Korn Chatikavanij has urged the government to investigate refinery profits rather than asking operators to donate excess earnings to help ease the economic burden from soaring energy costs.

title: Disaster declared in three northern Thai provinces
desc: Three provinces in northern Thailand — Chiang Mai, Lamphun and Phayao — have been declared emergency disaster zones after days of hazardous air quality, enabling provincial governors to disburse emergency funds to address the situation.

title: Molam, the heartbeat of Isan migrants
desc: Molam, a centuries-old musical tradition from northeastern Thailand (Isan) and the surrounding region, is finding firmer footing in Bangkok, where people who call Isan home make up the city’s largest migrant community.

title: No, this is not a New Normal
desc: “This is the New Normal” is how spokesperson for the Centre for Monitoring the Situation in the Middle East, Nuttaa Mahattana, has characterised Thailand’s current struggle with soaring fuel and commodities prices triggered by the war in the Middle East.
